meta.subject: Zambian Sign Language
meta.philosophy: The Lower Primary Syllabi has been reviewed by the need to provide a national age-appropriate curriculum for children at Lower Primary level. The syllabi aim to provide quality education that is aligned with the Competence-Based Curriculum and 21st Century Skills. It also aims to impart the knowledge, skills, values and positive attitudes that should enable children live and grow into productive and useful members of their communities and the Zambian society.
meta.competence_learning: The syllabus is for Literacy and Language in Zambian Sign Language (ZSL). This learning area has integrated necessary content for early learning stimulation and accomplishment of the child's developmental milestones. The content provided in this syllabus are aimed at facilitating holistic development of children. The suggested activities are designed to offer children hands-on experiences through manipulation of real objects, interaction with nature and learning through play. Thus, the children will develop knowledge, desirable lifelong skills, values and positive attitudes needed for their personal and national development. Cross cutting themes and emerging issues have been incorporated in the syllabus to ensure that children cultivate a mindset, skills, values and positive attitudes that prepare them to live responsible lives and be protected from life threatening vices.
goals: To provide quality education aligned with the Competence-Based Curriculum and 21st Century Skills.
To develop the learners' competency in Zambian Sign Language to facilitate learning across the curriculum.
To consolidate observing, signing, reading and writing skills introduced at Early Childhood Education level.
To facilitate holistic development of children including knowledge, desirable lifelong skills, values and positive attitudes.
To ensure children cultivate a mindset, skills, values and positive attitudes that prepare them to live responsible lives.
To allow for a smooth transition from Grade 1 to Grade 2 learning.

specific_outcomes: Use appropriate vocabulary to discuss composition of an extended family
Identify members of an extended family using pictures
Describe seasons of the year using appropriate vocabulary
Mention seasons of the year in Zambia (COLD SEASON, DRY SEASON, RAINY SEASON)
Use appropriate vocabulary to name places found in the community
Use appropriate vocabulary to identify good practices in the community
Observe instructions and respond
Identify the occupations found in the community
Use appropriate language to express feelings
Use appropriate sign language to express personal opinions
Describe wild animals using appropriate vocabulary
Demonstrate good manners and behaviour
Discuss respect, honesty and empathy based on a text
Discuss good behaviour (GREET, LANGUAGE GOOD USE, PLEASE, SORRY, EXCUSE ME, THANK YOU)
Analyse simple stories
Observe signing of simple traditional stories on different themes (animals, legends)
Retell simple stories (animals, legends)
Answer questions based on the story: main characters, setting, plot and important events
Depict elements of simple plays
Dramatise simple plays observing parameters of sign, stage movement, signing confidence
Answer questions based on the play: theme, plot, setting, characterisation
Record simple plays in playful situations
Sign simple poems
Sign simple poems on cross-cutting issues (Cholera, good practices in the community)
Sing simple sign language songs

Observe and sing simple songs (National Anthem, Hymns, praise and worship songs, Road Safety Songs, Alphabet Song, Twinkle Twinkle Little Star, Head Shoulders Knees and Toes, If You're Happy and You Know It, Five Little Monkeys Jumping on the bed)
Answer questions based on the song
Use appropriate vocabulary to describe people
Describe people using appropriate adjectives (SHORT, FAT, SLIM)
Use appropriate language in playing games
Play language games (Simon Says, story chase, spy, what am I)
Read words with consonant blends/clusters
Identify initial consonant blends (pl, sl, bl, r, cr, dr, gr, fr, pr, tr, sc, sm, tw) and final consonant blends (lb, ld, lf, lk, lm, ln, lp, lt, ft, nt, pt, xt)
Read words with consonant blends (plate, slow, brown, bulb, half, left)
Read sight words in sentences with automaticity
Recognise sight words (COME, SOME, HERE, THERE, GO, NOON, LY, YOU, ONE, MY, HAVE, LIVE, GIVE, LITTLE, DOWN)
Read sight words
Read sight words in sentences
Understand Subject-Verb (SV), Object-Verb (OV), Subject-Object-Verb (SOV), Object-Subject-Verb (OSV) sentence patterns in ZSL
Read text with understanding
Read simple stories, tales, fables, traditional stories, fairy tales
Retell simple stories, sequencing events in a story
Answer questions based on the story: main characters, setting and important events
Answer comprehension questions (WH Questions)
Explore a variety of reading materials
Carry out book reviews, retelling and identifying characters of the story
Demonstrate legible formation of both lower and upper case letters
Practise letter formation: lower case (ascenders: b, d, l, f, h, t, k; descenders: g, j, p, q, y; square letters: a, c, e, i, m, n, o, r, u, w, x)
Practise letter formation of upper case letters: ascenders A-Z
Demonstrate legible writing of words
Practise writing words: decodable and sight words carrying ascenders, descenders and square letters (BAG, BRIDGE, FLY)
Demonstrate legible writing of phrases
Demonstrate correct writing of sentences
Write words, phrases and simple sentences independently
Write words independently using a picture or real object
Fill in crossword puzzles (across and down words)
Write phrases based on pictures and real objects independently
Write sentences based on pictures such as conversation posters and picture strips independently
Sequence words according to alphabetical order
Arrange given words in alphabetical order
Sequence sentences logically
Rearrange sentences logically
Use spelling rules
Complete given words by writing missing letters/graphemes
Play spelling games
Search for words in a text that follow a certain spelling rule
Keep a spelling journal
Write dictated words (DOG, CAT, BOY, CHURCH)
Write dictated phrases (FLOWER RED, MAN TALL)
Write dictated sentences
Demonstrate word relationships in synonyms
Map given words with their synonyms (LARGE-BIG, SMALL-LITTLE)
Match words with similar meanings (FAR-DISTANT, GOOD-FINE, HARD-DIFFICULT)
Sort words into categories of synonyms (BIG, LARGE, HUGE, GIANT)
Demonstrate word relationships in antonyms
Map given words with their antonyms (LARGE-SMALL, LITTLE-THIN)
Match words with opposite meanings (FAR-NEAR)
Sort words into categories of antonyms
Draw pictures to depict antonyms
Use nouns
Circle proper nouns from a list of words
Write two or three nouns found in the classroom
Create a list of nouns found in a picture
Write a short story using at least two to three different nouns
Use duplication to show plural nouns
Apply knowledge of using verbs
Identify verbs depicted in pictures
Write sentences with action words
Use prepositions correctly
Identify prepositions: position of things in given sentences (IN, ON, UNDER, AT, NEXT, BEHIND, FRONT)
Use prepositions in simple sentences
Use conjunctions (but, and)
Identify conjunctions in given sentences
Write two simple sentences and join them using 'but' or 'and'
Use possessive pronouns
Identify possessive pronouns in given sentences (MY, MINE, YOUR)
Use correct pronouns in the blank spaces in given sentences
Use punctuation marks in sentences
Punctuate a list of items with a comma
Add question marks to interrogative statements
Use punctuation marks in given text: capital letters, full stop, spacing, question mark, comma
